paper_title: Verified Detection and Prevention of Concurrency Anomalies in Multi-Agent Large Language Model Systems

claims.claim: Five concurrency anomalies identified and formalized in TLA+ (A1, A2, A3, A4 deferred, A6)

evidence.detail: A1 (Stale-Generation): Definition 1 — predicate over history with ∃ agents, cells, times satisfying read-while-write condition
A2 (Phantom-Tool): Definition 2 — predicate over tool registry mutations during generation phase
A3 (Causal-Cascade): Definition 3 — predicate over predecessor closure with abort flag
A6 (Tool-Effect Reordering): Definition 4 — predicate over issuance order vs commit order
A1: 5-state counterexample at bounds (3 agents, 2 cells, 8 slots)
A2: 4-state counterexample at same bounds
A3: 2-operation minimal witness (commit + abort + dependent read)
A6: 3-state counterexample at same bounds

claims.claim: A consistency lattice (Boolean lattice over {A1,A2,A3,A6}) with 16 points and a chosen 5-point chain L0-L4

evidence.detail: Lattice: 2^4 = 16 points, each point = subset of anomalies excluded; order by set inclusion
Chosen chain: L0=⊤, L1=-A1, L2=-A1∩A3, L3=-A1∩A3∩A6, L4=-A1∩A2∩A3∩A6
11 theorems in Hierarchy.tla: 4 adjacent containments, 6 transitive soundness, 1 aggregate; 15 obligations, all discharged by definitional unfolding
A1 structural lower bound proved (A1LowerBound.tla, 28 obligations)

claims.claim: Detector pipelines verified sound and complete in Verus against TLA+ specifications

evidence.detail: 24 obligations for detector equivalence chain; detect_a1 two-sided equivalence includes triply-nested existential
soundness and completeness for detect_a2, detect_a3 (cascade residue), detect_a6
Two-record A3-satisfying history exhibited to confirm spec satisfiability

claims.claim: SSI (serializable snapshot isolation) prevents A1 at no statistically detectable token cost overhead

evidence.detail: SSI eliminates A1 at 100% (edit-review, plan-execute), 97% (triage); 900 sessions across gpt-4o and Claude Sonnet 4.5
Between-session cost comparison: SSI CI overlaps vanilla in every cell; paired design resolves ≤1 token overhead on one workload
Within-model wall-clock (gpt-4o, 270 sessions): SSI overhead at noise floor, single-digit-percent, all CIs overlap vanilla
SSI cost-parity replicated on Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Finding 4)

claims.claim: Pessimistic locking overhead is bounded (1.6x gpt-4o, 2.3x Claude), not order-of-magnitude

evidence.detail: gpt-4o worst-case 62% overhead (triage, Finding 2); overall worst 1.6x vanilla
Claude Sonnet 4.5 worst-case 124% overhead (triage); overall 2.3x vanilla
Pessimistic wall-clock overhead at noise floor on most workloads; max directional in triage but CIs overlap
Pessimistic locking drops 40.7% of synthetic operations; 100% A1 prevention

claims.claim: Causal-cascade (A3) is prevented by the verified L2 runtime; live agent loop with real LLMs confirms

evidence.detail: lemma_l2_reachable_no_a3 proves no reachable state admits A3 in L2Runtime
l2_causal.rs: unguarded baseline exhibits A3 at every chain depth; causal runtime exhibits 0%
gpt-4o-mini, claude-haiku-4-5, Llama-3.2: supervisor-driven retraction varied sharply (8-79%), but runtime prevented A3 in 100% of retracted sessions
lib_l2_exec.rs: 49 obligations, 0 errors, 0 axioms, exec-mode refinement with no added trust base entries

claims.claim: Tool-effect reordering (A6) exists in LangGraph's ToolNode; commit-order sequencer removes it

evidence.detail: ToolNode uses asyncio.gather; gpt-4o-mini batched 5-tool pipeline in 61% of turns, reordered in 83% of batched turns
langchain-ai/langgraphjs issue #861 confirms user requirement for sequential tool execution for stateful tools
Theorem g (lib_l3_sequencer.rs): commit-order sequencer orders effects by issuance time; cor_sequencer_no_a6 eliminates A6
Sequenced ToolNode: 0% reordering on every model-emitted batch (rule-of-three CI [0, 0.37])
gather preserves ToolMessage order by input position — A6 invisible in transcript

claims.claim: Snapshot-insufficiency observation: a structural snapshot definition does not exclude A1 (A1 ≠ snapshot guarantee)

evidence.detail: 1,934 states, depth 5: history with structural snapshot condition (read at time 0, commit at time 2) yet A1 fires (agent writes at time 1)

claims.claim: Total: 274 curated distinct verified proof obligations, 0 errors, across all Verus modules

evidence.detail: 24 (detector_equivalence) + 40 (safety theorems) + 84 (refinements) + 9 (concurrent) + 22 (l2_safety) + 27 (l2_exec) + 9 (a4) + 4 (rustbelt) + 6 (l3_safety) + 5 (l3_sequencer) + 5 (l4_safety) + 7 (langgraph) + 8 (occ) + 5 (l3_exec) + 9 (l4_exec) + 7 (lib_langgraph) + 5 (l1_lower_bound) = 274
2 foundational axioms (string-identifier injectivity, null-sentinel mapping) + 3 RustBelt stubs (lock_is_acquire, drop_is_release, event_seqcst)

claims.claim: deer-flow silent lost update (issue #3123) is a production instance of A1; reducer fix formalized as a verified L2 refinement

evidence.detail: Artifact reproduces #3123 on unmodified LangGraph runtime using deer-flow's own reducer; without reducer, 2 seeded todos silently lost
lib_langgraph_refinement.rs (7 verified, 0 axioms): additive channel value = initial + concatenation of every payload — no committed contribution ever lost
INVALID_CONCURRENT_GRAPH_UPDATE occurs in 34 third-party repositories including widely-starred projects
Letta: 18/24 concurrent appends silently lost (Section 6.5); AutoGen actor model avoids shared-mutable-state class entirely

claims.claim: Verus spec–runtime refinement closes gap between abstract safety theorems and deployed Rust code

evidence.detail: 4 refinement files covering pessimistic-locking (31 obligations), SSI projection (18), SSI version-chain (17), default-SI (18)
SI validation gate (validate: 5 verified), pessimistic acquisition check (can_acquire: 5 verified), detector (9 verified)
axiom_string_to_int_injective and axiom_null_sentinel — both structural encoding properties, not domain claims

claims.claim: 80%+ A1 firing rate in cookbook-derived scenarios on unmodified frameworks (LangGraph RoundRobinGroupChat, AutoGen)

evidence.detail: 6 scenarios from public LangGraph/AutoGen examples; 0/500 A1 for stateless scenarios; 90/100 for typed-shared-state (any-agent); 89/100 (cross-agent)

claims.claim: Operational materiality of A1 is role-dependent: reviewer diverges 100%, engineer 54.5%, editor 0%

evidence.detail: Controlled task: triage priority, 75% materiality (95% CI [68.4%, 80.8%])
Reviewer (assess doc): 100% divergence (95% CI [91.8%, 100%]); engineer (resolve): 54.5% [36.4%, 71.9%]; editor (author): 0% [0%, 3.6%]
Blind sample of 60: LLM judge agreed with human on 59/60 (Cohen's κ=0.96, 95% CI [0.90, 1.00])
